entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop important in the semi-arid tropics of Asia, Africa and Latin America, used for food, feed, fodder and bioenergy

entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall where sorghum is predominantly grown

entities.description: A biological mechanism that enables easy cross-pollination in sorghum

entities.description: A biological mechanism that enables easy cross-pollination in sorghum

entities.description: Sorghum varieties developed through pollination control, similar to self-pollinated crops

entities.description: Sorghum populations developed through cross-pollination

entities.description: Traits targeted for genetic enhancement to stabilize crop performance against stresses

entities.description: Modern scientific tools used to complement conventional crop improvement in sorghum

entities.name: SEED PRODUCTION

entities.description: The study of the timing of biological events in the sorghum life cycle

entities.description: The variety of genetic resources available for sorghum improvement

entities.description: Theories regarding hybrid vigor used in sorghum breeding
